name: simpleindex
perf_notes: Tiny Starlette + uvicorn ASGI app (single process by default). Local file (path) routes read the entire distribution into memory with path.read_bytes() per request — no streaming, no Range, no caching. HTTP routes are a single 302 redirect to upstream, so the client downloads directly from PyPI/upstream and simpleindex itself does no byte proxying. No published benchmarks; intended for small/personal indexes.
scale_notes: No built-in HA, clustering, or multi-node support. The server is effectively stateless (it reads files from disk or emits redirects), so multiple instances could in principle sit behind a load balancer, but nothing in the project supports or documents this. Designed for a single small private/personal index, not large-scale operation.
summary: A ~130-line PEP 503 simple-index server that maps package-name patterns to local directories or upstream 302 redirects via a TOML routing config — a minimal personal/private index, not a full repository manager.
extras: Core idea is a 'virtual index from routing rules': TOML [routes."pattern"] blocks map package-name patterns (with {var} substitution) to either a local directory (source = "path") or an upstream URL (source = "http"); first matching route wins

Pluggable custom route types via the 'simpleindex.routes' entry-point group — subclass simpleindex.routes.Route and implement async get_page()/get_file(); README ships an Amazon S3 source as the documented example, installed via 'pipx inject'

HTTP routes are implemented purely as 302 redirects to the upstream simple index (e.g. https://pypi.org/simple/{project}/) — no caching, mirroring, or byte proxying; the client follows the redirect and downloads from upstream
Config file supports a 'path.toml::key.path' selector so the configuration can be embedded under a key inside another TOML file (e.g. pyproject.toml)

The [server] section is passed straight through as kwargs to uvicorn.run(), so any uvicorn option (host, port, workers, ssl_keyfile/ssl_certfile) can be set — undocumented but functional, including TLS
Read-only by design: package files are placed on disk manually; there is no upload, deletion, yank, or management API
Path routes only list filenames that parse as valid wheels or sdists via packaging.utils; anything else (incl. eggs) is silently ignored
Generated HTML is bare PEP 503 anchors only ({name}) — no data-requires-python, no #sha256= hashes, no data-yanked, no repository-version meta tag
